replies.msg:
Dear MTR Corporation,

I am writing to complain about a series of unsatisfactory services rendered by MTR Corporation (MTRC) over the past two months, especially for its decision to close various MTR stations on 24th and 25th August. As a tax-paying citizen in Hong Kong, I am extremely disappointed by how MTRC delivers its service which result in its failure to protect passengers, its fellow colleagues and citizens.


Firstly, MTRC closed various MTR stations on 24th and 25th August which resulted in unnecessary disruption to citizens’ daily life. Its underlying aim is to cause inconvenience to citizens who were going to join the protest. On 24th August, at 10:27am, MTRC announced that it would suspend service from Choi Hung Station to Tiu Keng Leng Station and close Kowloon Bay, Ngau Tau Kok, Kwun Tong and Lam Tin station starting from 12pm. On 25th August, at 11:30am, it announced that it would suspend service in and close Kwai Fong, Tsuen Wan and Tsuen Wan West stations starting from 1:30pm. As a public transportation, this is totally unacceptable. On 24th August, there was no violence inside the mtr stations. The protest was a lawful one which was approved by the police. The protest started at 1:30pm which did not even start yet when MTRC announced the closure of those stations. MTRC should know that many citizens rely on mtr for transportation and it caused huge inconvenience to citizens who lived in the districts nearby. As a responsible corporation, MTRC should not disregard the interest of the public and close the stations whenever it wants. Moreover, MTRC used its trains to drive a bunch of police to various areas. Why you would provide service to HK police while refusing to do so to ordinary citizens? Please provide a satisfactory response. This is very irresponsible and such action is outrageous. This clearly shows that MTRC is cooperating with HKSAR government to deter people from joining the protest. Closing stations is a political means to suppress citizens' right of political participation. I do not know what pressure or threat HKSAR and/or HK police put on your company, but under no circumstances should you commit such cooperation to further the government’s political aim. I would like to reiterate that the protest is totally lawful and there is no reason to close many stations before the protest even started.

28/8．31/8會出動既手足記住幫手宣傳埋1/9機場

[9．1機場] 文宣集合 2.0
https://lih.kg/1507880

現有文宣集合 (Google Drive)
https://drive.google.com/open?id=1GCRs7ie1gwrmV6-388fnE5PnFq_jnbwe

replies.msg:
Secondly, until now MTRC still has not provided a satisfactory response to us regarding the incidents at Kwai Fong and Tai Koo station on 11th August and Yuen Long station on 21st July. MTRC merely told us that it is concerned about police enforcing their duties inside the stations and would communicate with the police. Hong Kong citizens do not just want you to be concerned about this. You say that you emphasize on the safety of passengers and colleagues. Therefore, you should not be indifferent to what the police did on 11th August. They released tear gas rounds and shot pepper ball bullets at citizens inside Kwai Fong station and Tai Koo station respectively. Apart from carrying out work to thoroughly remove the tear gas inside the station, MTRC should condemn the police of committing such dangerous and brutal attacks inside the mtr stations, instead of being tolerant, or even agreeing with such actions.

On 21st July, many innocent passengers were attacked by the triad members inside Yuen Long station while MTRC management board ordered the train captain to stop the train. MTRC did nothing about such large-scale terrorist attack in the station. Many innocent citizens, whether coming home after joining the 21st July protest or not, were seriously injured in the incident. Even HK police refused to protect HK citizens by cooperating with the gangs, MTRC could still try to minimize the casualty by providing additional trains to carry passengers to a safe area, rather than doing nothing and allowing gangs to enter into the train and randomly attack others.

replies.msg:
MTRC said that it values the safety of passengers and colleagues, and it should therefore act in accordance with it.

Not to mention the injunction that MTRC obtained against HK citizens, the closure of Sha Tin station on 14th July by cooperating with the police, the constant malfunction of mtr trains, the rising fare every year, what MTRC has done over the past few months are genuinely disappointing and unacceptable.

In the end, I would like to emphasize that MTRC is a HK public corporation, it should do its best to serve and protect the interest of HK citizens, but not succumb to the pressure from HKSAR government and/or China. HK citizens are not supposed to be treated in such a deplorable way by your company.

Being a corporation with a conscience, I would like MTRC to provide us a satisfactory response, including condemning the actions of the police and apologizing to HK citizens, and any actions that you would take to address the above-mentioned problems by 31st August 2019. Do not just put your fellow employees into the centre of attention while you yourself hiding somewhere else. As employees of your company, they deserve your respect and protection.

Yours truly,
Name

九月一號下午一點機場見
